title: Zuppa di pesce
subtitle: Italian mixed seafood soup with tomato and white wine
excerpt: Italian fish soup in the brodetto tradition: white fish, mussels, and prawns cooked quickly in a tomato and white wine broth aromatic with garlic, chilli, and parsley. The broth is as important as the seafood. Serve with grilled bread rubbed with garlic.
sourceNotes: Zuppa di pesce in the brodetto style is documented throughout the Adriatic and Tyrrhenian coasts of Italy, with regional variants differing in the type of tomato, the presence of saffron, and the specific seafood used. Artusi includes a Livornese fish soup (cacciucco) and related preparations in La scienza in cucina (1891, public domain). The brodetto tradition (fish cooked briefly in an acidic, aromatic broth) is pan-Italian coastal cooking.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The tomato broth base can be made up to 2 days ahead. Add the seafood only when ready to serve.

content.text: Italian fish soup is not about technique so much as sequencing. The broth comes first and has to be good: aromatic, acidic, well-seasoned, deep with tomato and white wine. The seafood goes in at the very end and cooks briefly. Overcooked fish in a mediocre broth is what most fish soups are. Perfectly cooked fish in an excellent broth is what this is trying to be.

Use at least three types of seafood for variety. Mussels open and flavour the broth with their liquor. Firm white fish holds its shape. Prawns add sweetness. The specific combination is flexible: clams, squid, scallops — add what is fresh. The broth is the constant.

items.prepNote: firm white fish (cod, monkfish, sea bass), cut into 4 cm pieces
stalks finely chopped, leaves roughly chopped

content.text: Warm the {{olive-oil}} of olive oil in a wide, heavy pot over medium heat. Add the {{onion}} onion and parsley stalks. Cook for 8 minutes until the onion is soft and pale gold. Add the {{garlic}} of garlic and chilli flakes and cook for 1 minute. Pour in the {{white-wine-dry}} of white wine and let it bubble for 2 minutes. Add the {{tinned-tomatoes}} of tomatoes and 300 ml of water. Season with salt. Simmer for 15 minutes until the broth is flavourful and slightly reduced.

Add the {{mussels}} to the broth. Cover and cook for 3 minutes until the mussels begin to open. Add the pieces of {{cod-fillet}} and the {{prawns-raw}} prawns. Cover and cook for 4 to 5 more minutes until the fish is just opaque and the prawns are pink. Discard any mussels that have not opened.

Scatter the parsley leaves over the soup and drizzle with the {{extra-virgin-olive-oil}} of extra-virgin olive oil. Taste for salt. Serve immediately in warm bowls with grilled bread rubbed with a raw garlic clove.

items.symptom: Fish has fallen apart in the broth
items.cause: Added too early, or broth simmering too vigorously
items.fix: Fish goes in after the mussels have started opening; keep the broth at a gentle simmer, not a rolling boil

items.cause: Not enough reduction time before the seafood went in
items.fix: Simmer the tomato broth for the full 15 minutes before adding any seafood; it should coat a spoon lightly

items.cause: Overcooked; prawns need only 3 to 4 minutes in a hot broth
items.fix: Add prawns at the same time as the fish; remove from heat as soon as they turn pink throughout

items.symptom: Soup tastes flat despite good seafood
items.cause: Finishing olive oil skipped; broth underseasoned
items.fix: The extra-virgin olive oil at the end is not garnish — it rounds and binds the flavour; taste and season just before serving

content.text: Every port town in Italy has a version of fish soup with a different name and a fierce local attachment to its specific form. The brodetto of the Adriatic coast from Ancona to Bari, the cacciucco of Livorno, the ciuppin of Genoa, the buridda of Sardinia: all are the same impulse expressed differently, which is to make a meal from the fish that the nets brought in and the tomatoes and wine that were cheap. The version here takes no specific regional position; it is fish soup as a method rather than a recipe, which allows it to work with whatever is in the fishmonger's that day.
